text: We identified azabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes (aza-BCHs) and bicyclo[1.1.1]pentanes (BCPs) as a rich set of *sp*³-rich motifs, commonly used as three dimensional bioisoster for aromatic systems to impart greater metabolic stability to lead compounds. Methods to “scaffold hop” and directly interconvert these cores through single atom skeletal editing are scarce. Herein we establish a formal nitrogen deletion route connecting the two cores. We utilized photochemical [2+2] cycloadditions to generate multifunctionalized aza-BCHs, which were subsequently treated with a deamination catalyst to give bridge functionalized BCPs. BCPs with complex substitution patterns are typically hard to synthesize, making this method highly versatile for the generation of privileged bicyclic cores relevant to the pharmaceutical industry.
explanation: The text uses polished, domain-specific chemistry language that could come from either a human writer or an advanced AI. Its clarity and technical detail make it plausible as AI-generated, but equally plausible as a human-authored abstract.
